behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real filesystem watcher recursion behavior (OS events + watcher library configuration), not just local function logic. MOCK-HIDE applies: synthetic in-process events can pass even if real recursive watching fails on actual OS behavior. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory under the watched root, write a file into that subdirectory, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory for that nested file path.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: The behavior is primarily module handoff/state (event intake -> dedup store/window -> scheduler), and can be caught with in-process wiring plus controllable clock/time-window config. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: no required real OS/network/subprocess for this specific assertion, no AI/ML output dependency, no multi-agent sequence. Rejected System because behavior text is about dedup policy correctness, not OS-specific event semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the daemon pipeline in-process with a fake clock, submit two processing requests for the same normalized file path within the configured window, assert only one pipeline execution occurs; then advance clock past window and assert a new request is accepted.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Stays above Unit because the real risk is cross-module state handoff (request intake, dedup cache, scheduler), not a single pure function.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly cites OS multi-event emission; real failure appears at OS watcher boundary. MOCK-HIDE applies strongly: mocked single-event or simplified event streams can miss duplicate-event races. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML or agent chain in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Run daemon against a real watched directory, perform a write pattern known to trigger multiple FS notifications (create + modify/rename sequence), and verify one and only one processing result is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Requires real OS filesystem lifecycle behavior (directory deletion/recreation, watcher handle invalidation/rebind). In-process fakes often miss descriptor invalidation and re-subscription edge cases. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML dependency or multi-agent steps in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete the watched directory while daemon keeps running, recreate it at the same path, place a new file, and assert processing resumes without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering depends on real filesystem timestamp semantics/granularity under concurrent arrivals plus watcher/event queue interplay. Mocked metadata can hide real ordering bugs caused by identical/low-resolution timestamps and race timing. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML or agent pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real watched directory, create multiple files near-simultaneously with controlled creation times (or staggered writes within timestamp resolution constraints), then assert processing order matches creation timestamp ordering rule.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem/write-timing condition (partial writes, flush timing, event timing) that appears at OS I/O boundary. In-process mocks usually cannot reproduce true partial-write race behavior, so MOCK-HIDE applies. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI/ML decision or multi-agent sequence in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, begin writing a large file in chunks with delays, trigger arrival event early, verify daemon does not process partial content, then verify it processes once after write completion.
